values.content: You're an intelligent bot perfect at creating cover letters for a job. Please take the candidates resume and create a customized cover letter to the job.

=Here's the job listing details:
Title: {{ $('Get dataset items').item.json.title }}
Description: {{ $('Get dataset items').item.json.descriptionText }}
Name: {{ $('Get dataset items').item.json.companyName }}
Location: {{ $('Get dataset items').item.json.location }}

Here is the candidates skill set:
📄 SAMPLE RESUME

Alex Morgan
📍 Toronto, ON, Canada | 📧 alex.morgan@email.com | 📞 (123) 456-7890 | 🌐 linkedin.com/in/alexmorgan | github.com/alexmseo (optional if portfolio on GitHub)

🎓 Education
Bachelor of Business Administration – Marketing
University of Toronto, Toronto, ON
Graduated: April 2025 | GPA: 3.7/4.0

Relevant coursework:

Digital Marketing Strategies

Data Analytics in Marketing

Consumer Behaviour

SEO & Content Marketing (elective)

💼 Experience
SEO Intern
GrowthRocket Agency, Remote | Jan 2025 – Apr 2025

Conducted technical SEO audits for 10+ client websites using tools like Screaming Frog, Ahrefs, and Google Search Console.

Implemented structured data (Schema.org) to improve click-through rates in search results.

Optimized on-page elements such as meta titles, descriptions, H1-H3 tags, internal linking, and image alt text.

Collaborated with the content team to develop keyword-rich blog content and updated outdated pages.

Improved page speed performance using tools like PageSpeed Insights and GTmetrix.

Freelance SEO Consultant
Self-Employed | May 2024 – Present

Helped 3 small local businesses optimize their websites for search engines, increasing organic traffic by 30–70% within 3 months.

Conducted keyword research and competitive analysis to guide content creation.

Set up Google Analytics 4 and Google Search Console for performance monitoring.

🛠 Skills
SEO Tools: Google Search Console, GA4, Ahrefs, Screaming Frog, SEMrush, Yoast SEO
CMS: WordPress, Wix
Technical SEO: Site audits, page speed optimization, canonical tags, indexing & crawling
On-Page SEO: Meta tags, internal linking, content structure, image optimization
Soft Skills: Communication, problem-solving, attention to detail, teamwork

📚 Certifications
Google Analytics Certification – Google

SEO Fundamentals – Semrush Academy

Technical SEO Certification – Moz (or any other you’ve done)

=You're an intelligent bot rating how closely a job listing is to a candidates skill set, on a score of 10. Please add up all the points and create a total out of 10.
a) 4 points for my resume is an amazing fit for the job, 2 point for mostly matching
b) 3 point if the job is paying over $25/hr, $4,000/month or $50,000/year
c) 1 point for it being a remote position (should equal true)
e) 1 point if the company offers benefits (any benefits)
f) 1 point if it's full time (not contract or part-time)

For example:
If the job fits mostly, and pays over $25/hr, and is remote, but has no benefits and is not full-time, the total points would be: 6
###
If the job fits perfectly, but is under $50,000/year, isn't a remote position, has a few benefits, and isn't full-time, the total points would be: 5

=Here's the job listing details:
Salary: {{ $('Get dataset items').item.json.salary.salaryText }}
Remote: {{ $('Get dataset items').item.json.isRemote }}
Benefits: {{ $('Get dataset items').item.json.benefits }}
Job type/term: {{ $json.jobType[0] }} (i.e. part time, full time, contract etc)
{{ $json.descriptionText }}

Implemented structured data (Schema.org) to improve click-through rates in search results.

Optimized on-page elements such as meta titles, descriptions, H1-H3 tags, internal linking, and image alt text.

Collaborated with the content team to develop keyword-rich blog content and updated outdated pages.

Improved page speed performance using tools like PageSpeed Insights and GTmetrix.

Freelance SEO Consultant
Self-Employed | May 2024 – Present

Helped 3 small local businesses optimize their websites for search engines, increasing organic traffic by 30–70% within 3 months.

Conducted keyword research and competitive analysis to guide content creation.

Set up Google Analytics 4 and Google Search Console for performance monitoring.

json.title: Commission-Based Marketing Representative
json.descriptionHtml:
Job Overview
We are seeking a dynamic and results-driven Marketing Representative to join our team. The ideal candidate will possess a strong understanding of various marketing strategies and techniques, including SEO, digital marketing, and content marketing. This role requires creativity, analytical skills, and the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously. As a Marketing Representative, you will play a crucial role in promoting our brand and driving sales through effective marketing campaigns.

Job Description & Responsibilities:

As a Marketing Representative, you will be the face of our firm in the community. You’ll be responsible for driving client acquisition, building referral networks, and promoting our financial services to business owners, entrepreneurs, and professionals in the region.

Key responsibilities include:

  • Prospect and generate leads through direct outreach, referrals, networking events, business meetups, cold calling, email outreach, and social platforms like LinkedIn
  • Educate and inform potential clients about the value of outsourced financial services (e.g., CFO services, tax planning, bookkeeping)
  • Develop and maintain strong relationships with local business owners, real estate professionals, attorneys, CPAs, and other potential referral sources
  • Schedule consultations or introductions for the internal team to close (you’re the door-opener)
  • Collaborate with internal team members to refine messaging and target outreach strategies
  • Attend local business events, chamber of commerce meetings, and other networking opportunities to represent the firm professionally
  • Maintain a CRM or lead tracker to monitor outreach, follow-ups, and conversion metrics
  • Report weekly on outreach activity, lead progress, and marketing insights from the field
  • Stay informed on basic financial service offerings, market trends, and local business news to have informed conversations with prospects
  • Represent the brand with integrity and professionalism at all times

What You’ll Do:

  • Promote our full suite of financial services to small businesses, entrepreneurs, and professionals
  • Identify and engage with potential clients through outreach, networking, referrals, and local events
  • Educate prospects on the benefits of working with a professional CFO/accounting team
  • Develop and execute simple local marketing strategies (digital or in-person)
  • Represent our brand professionally and ethically in all interactions
  • Report on lead generation and conversion activities

Experience

  • Proven experience in digital marketing, content marketing, or related fields.
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills
  • Familiarity with SEO best practices and social media marketing strategies.
  • Knowledge of performance marketing techniques and analytics tools such as Google AdWords.
  • Proficiency in WordPress for website management and content updates.
  • Strong copywriting skills with the ability to create persuasive messaging for diverse audiences.
  • Experience in product management and budgeting for marketing initiatives is a plus.
  • Self-motivated, disciplined, and goal-oriented
  • Comfortable working independently with minimal supervision
  • Knowledge of the Pittsburgh small business landscape is a plus
  • Ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced environment while managing multiple projects effectively. If you are passionate about marketing and eager to make an impact within our organization, we encourage you to apply for this exciting opportunity.

Job Type: Contract

Pay: $3,000.00 - $5,000.00 per month

Benefits:

  • Flexible schedule

Work Location: Remote
json.descriptionText:
*Job Overview*
We are seeking a dynamic and results-driven Marketing Representative to join our team. The ideal candidate will possess a strong understanding of various marketing strategies and techniques, including SEO, digital marketing, and content marketing. This role requires creativity, analytical skills, and the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously. As a Marketing Representative, you will play a crucial role in promoting our brand and driving sales through effective marketing campaigns.

*Job Description & Responsibilities:*

As a Marketing Representative, you will be the face of our firm in the community. You’ll be responsible for driving client acquisition, building referral networks, and promoting our financial services to business owners, entrepreneurs, and professionals in the region.

Key responsibilities include:

* Prospect and generate leads through direct outreach, referrals, networking events, business meetups, cold calling, email outreach, and social platforms like LinkedIn

* Educate and inform potential clients about the value of outsourced financial services (e.g., CFO services, tax planning, bookkeeping)

* Develop and maintain strong relationships with local business owners, real estate professionals, attorneys, CPAs, and other potential referral sources

* Schedule consultations or introductions for the internal team to close (you’re the door-opener)

* Collaborate with internal team members to refine messaging and target outreach strategies

* Attend local business events, chamber of commerce meetings, and other networking opportunities to represent the firm professionally

* Maintain a CRM or lead tracker to monitor outreach, follow-ups, and conversion metrics

* Report weekly on outreach activity, lead progress, and marketing insights from the field

* Stay informed on basic financial service offerings, market trends, and local business news to have informed conversations with prospects

* Represent the brand with integrity and professionalism at all times

*What You’ll Do:*

* Promote our full suite of financial services to small businesses, entrepreneurs, and professionals

* Identify and engage with potential clients through outreach, networking, referrals, and local events

* Educate prospects on the benefits of working with a professional CFO/accounting team

* Develop and execute simple local marketing strategies (digital or in-person)

* Represent our brand professionally and ethically in all interactions

* Report on lead generation and conversion activities

*Experience*

* Proven experience in digital marketing, content marketing, or related fields.
* Strong communication and interpersonal skills
* Familiarity with SEO best practices and social media marketing strategies.
* Knowledge of performance marketing techniques and analytics tools such as Google AdWords.
* Proficiency in WordPress for website management and content updates.
* Strong copywriting skills with the ability to create persuasive messaging for diverse audiences.
* Experience in product management and budgeting for marketing initiatives is a plus.
* Self-motivated, disciplined, and goal-oriented
* Comfortable working independently with minimal supervision
* Knowledge of the Pittsburgh small business landscape is a plus
* Ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced environment while managing multiple projects effectively. If you are passionate about marketing and eager to make an impact within our organization, we encourage you to apply for this exciting opportunity.

Job Type: Contract

Pay: $3,000.00 - $5,000.00 per month

Benefits:
* Flexible schedule

Work Location: Remote

json.title: Real Estate Marketing Social Media Coordinator
json.descriptionHtml:
Are you ready to take your marketing career to the next level? We have an exciting opportunity for a Marketing Coordinator to join our growing real estate business.

The ideal candidate will have a well-developed capacity for coordinating and implementing marketing communication projects with responsibilities in social media, public relations, search marketing, advertising, brand stewardship and special events management.

In addition to marketing, brand, and social media related work, this position will also share a role in tracking sales data; maintaining collateral and marketing materials inventory; maintaining databases; and preparing reports.

We offer a great laid-back working environment, weekly team building meetings and a creative, fun atmosphere. If you’re ready to grow your career, come grow with us!
Compensation:

$37,500

Responsibilities:
  • Create, deliver and edit marketing materials and advertising campaigns that are effective and timely
  • Prepare signage and events for new community launches that will entice potential buyers
  • Plan and organize all team events, take photos, and then market on social media
  • Deliver social media content with a fresh approach and respond to all followers
  • Manage website design, content, SEO development and tracking analytics to ensure all content is up to date and relevant
Qualifications:
  • B.A. or B.S. in Marketing, Journalism, Business or related major required
  • Must possess excellent communication skills and have a positive, upbeat attitude
  • Expert in forward design concepts
  • Minimum 2 years of marketing/brand management experience in an agency or Real Estate sales environment
  • Maintains an open mind to new ideas and suggestions
About Company


We are an equal-opportunity employer. We welcome and encourage diversity in our workplace.

json.title: Marketing Manager
json.descriptionHtml:
We’re not your average real estate company.


At HomeBuyers of Pittsburgh, we’ve helped over 1,000 local families sell their homes with speed, trust, and care since 2015 — winning multiple local and national awards along the way while keeping the customer experience at the heart of everything we do.


At our brokerage, we’re redefining what it means to be a real estate agent. Our people get the tools, training, and culture to close more deals, earn more income, and love the work they do.


Together, we’re building brands that are shaking up the Pittsburgh market — and we’re just getting started.


Our mission is simple: help people win in real estate — whether that’s selling a house, building an investment portfolio, or launching a successful career. But just as important as what we do is how we do it.


We live our values every day:

  • Believe in Yourself, Your Team, and the Process
  • Over-Deliver for Our Customers
  • Be a Problem Solver, Take Initiative
  • No Bad Attitudes — Good Vibes Only
  • Collaboration Over Competition
  • Keep Learning and Improving
  • Always Do the Right Thing


We’re now hiring a Marketing Manager to bring energy, creativity, and organization to our growing brands. You’ll lead campaigns that drive motivated seller leads, fuel RealtyCo’s recruiting, and keep our personal brands front and center in Pittsburgh.


This is the role for someone who loves real estate, thrives in a fast-paced environment, and wants to make a measurable impact where their work truly matters.

Compensation:

$55,000 - $70,000 yearly

Responsibilities:
  • Manage direct mail campaigns: pulling lists, coordinating design/printing, tracking ROI.
  • Keep ads & SEO vendors accountable: review reports, flag issues, track KPIs.
  • Handle retargeting and online campaign alignment with offline marketing.
  • Run email recruiting campaigns (templates already built, just manage + improve).
  • Film & edit short-form content for Ryan & Joey (phone-level edits, reels, posts).
  • Maintain social media calendars and consistent posting across brands.
  • Collect testimonials, success stories, and repurpose them into content.
  • Coordinate with inside sales callers and marketing team members as needed.
Qualifications:
  • 2–4 years of marketing experience (agency, small business, or real estate background a plus).
  • Comfortable with social media, email tools, Canva, and basic video editing apps.
  • Organized, detail-oriented, and proactive — you don’t need hand-holding.
  • Strong writing skills (ads, posts, captions, emails).
  • Able to juggle multiple campaigns and vendors without losing quality.
About Company


We are the Home Buyers of Pittsburgh, a locally owned company that started in 2015 when two partners, Ryan Scialabba and Aaron Archibald, decided to join forces.

In the last ten years, we have increased from two partners to a full staff of 30, have bought and sold over 1,000 homes, and now have built a 200+ property portfolio.

We have a vertically integrated business that includes acquisitions, construction, financing, property management, and a brokerage.

For five years running, we've won TRIB Live's Best of The Best Real Estate Companies, PBT Best Places to Work 2x, and in 2020, we ranked #541 on the INC. 5000 list. In 2019, we won #1 Fastest Growing Company in Pittsburgh on the PITTSBURGH FAST 50 List and landed at #747 on the INC. 5000 List.

We're not just a company focused on revenue, but we're on a mission to help as many people as we can. We're a tight-knit, high-energy team that is always striving to get better.

json.title: E-Commerce Manager
json.descriptionHtml:
The E-Commerce Manager will be responsible for driving growth and delivering revenue goals for GAMMA Sports’ DTC business. This role will oversee the entire digital consumer journey, from building awareness at the top of the funnel with paid social and digital advertising, to optimizing conversions on the GAMMA Sports website. The ideal candidate is both strategic and hands-on, able to set an annual promotional and digital marketing strategy while also managing day-to-day execution, analytics, and cross-functional collaboration.


Key Responsibilities

  • Manage daily operations for Shopify and B2B digital storefronts.

  • Oversee full product setup: imagery, copy, SEO, tags, pricing, promotions.

  • Build and optimize collections, navigation, and landing pages for discoverability and conversion.

  • Own promotional strategy, execution and analytics.

  • Run QA across devices/browsers to ensure site speed, functionality, and accessibility.

  • Develop upsell, cross-sell, and bundling experiences to grow AOV and repeat rate.

  • Conduct keyword research and execute SEO strategies to improve rankings.

  • Optimize product listings and site content for search and conversion.

  • Track performance with GA4, Shopify Analytics, and SEO tools.

  • Own Klaviyo campaigns and automation: strategy, segmentation, testing, deliverability

  • Build and maintain automated email and SMS flows.

  • Partner with Marketing Coordinator on email/SMS content to drive engagement and revenue.

  • Develop digital ad strategies and oversee execution.

  • Manage loyalty programs to increase LTV.

  • Support affiliate programs through code development and management.

  • Track KPIs (traffic, conversion, AOV, retention, category performance) to deliver insights and recommendations to Marketing and Executive Leadership.

  • Translate data into strategies for site, CRM, promotions, and campaign optimization.

  • Directly manage a Marketing Coordinator to support campaign execution, merchandising, and content.

  • Collaborate cross-functionally with Marketing, Creative, Operations, and Sales to align DTC and B2B initiatives.

  • Manage external agencies/contractors (development, paid digital advertising).

Qualifications

  • 3-5 years of Shopify & CRM experience in a DTC brand or agency

  • Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, or related field.

  • Strong knowledge and hands-on experience in retail/digital marketing, including email campaigns, social media advertising, SEO, and conversion optimization.

  • Strong understanding of e-commerce metrics, product data, and customer behavior analytics.

  • Proficiency in Shopify, Klaviyo, GA4, and Excel

  • NetSuite ERP and/or Suite Commerce Experience a plus

  • Strong knowledge of HTML and CSS coding.

  • Data-driven mindset with the ability to translate analytics into strategy.

  • Experience managing direct reports.

  • Highly organized, detail-oriented, and adaptable in a fast-paced environment.

  • Excellent communication and cross-functional collaboration skills.

  • Passion for sports – especially tennis, pickleball or padel – is a big plus

What We Offer

  • Competitive salary

  • Comprehensive benefits package (health, dental, vision, 401k with match).

  • Paid Time Off

  • Parental Leave

  • Hybrid work environment with flexibility

  • Employee discounts on GAMMA Sports products.

  • Opportunity for professional growth in a dynamic, sports-focused company.


About GAMMA Sports

GAMMA Sports is a Pittsburgh-based, family-owned manufacturer with a 50-year legacy of innovating in racquet sport equipment, including tennis and pickleball. Best known for its patented breakthrough string technologies, GAMMA has leveraged its racquet sports expertise to develop high-quality paddles, balls, grips, and accessories that empower people who love to play at all levels. Providing equipment and education to help improve your game, the brand has become a trusted name among coaches, trainers, and racquet sport enthusiasts around the world.

oyZjNnSDMw

json.title: Digital Marketing Manager
json.descriptionHtml:
Loudon Motors Ford is seeking a creative, motivated, and results-driven Digital Marketing Manager to join our team. This role is ideal for someone who thrives on storytelling, analytics, and driving growth through engaging digital strategies.


If you are passionate about marketing, content creation, and community engagement and want to make an impact at a family-owned dealership with nearly 100 years of history, this may be the perfect fit.


Responsibilities:
  • Develop and manage the dealership’s digital marketing strategy across social media, website, email, and paid advertising
  • Create engaging content including graphics, videos, and copy for promotions, sales events, and community initiatives
  • Manage the dealership’s online reputation through customer reviews, Google Business Profile, and social engagement
  • Track and analyze marketing performance metrics to measure ROI and optimize campaigns
  • Support the sales team with lead generation, follow-up content, and promotional materials
  • Organize and promote dealership events including sales promotions, community drives, and seasonal campaigns
  • Maintain brand consistency while ensuring compliance with Ford Motor Company guidelines
  • Collaborate with leadership to develop creative campaigns that drive traffic and sales

Qualifications:
  • Experience in digital marketing, social media, or content creation (automotive experience a plus)
  • Bachelor's Degree in marketing, communications, journalism or related field
  • Strong skills in content writing, social media management, and brand storytelling
  • Experience with photo/video editing using Adobe, Canva, or similar tools
  • Familiarity with SEO, SEM, and Google Analytics
  • Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple projects at once
  • Creative mindset with the ability to generate fresh ideas and campaigns
  • A passion for customer engagement, community involvement, and automotive retail

Benefits:
  • Competitive pay based on experience
  • Health, dental, and vision insurance
  • 401(k) retirement plan
  • Paid time off and holidays
  • Employee discounts
  • Opportunity to be part of a long-standing, community-focused dealership
json.descriptionText:

If you are passionate about marketing, content creation, and community engagement and want to make an impact at a family-owned dealership with nearly 100 years of history, this may be the perfect fit.

Responsibilities:

• Create engaging content including graphics, videos, and copy for promotions, sales events, and community initiatives
• Manage the dealership’s online reputation through customer reviews, Google Business Profile, and social engagement
• Track and analyze marketing performance metrics to measure ROI and optimize campaigns
• Support the sales team with lead generation, follow-up content, and promotional materials
• Organize and promote dealership events including sales promotions, community drives, and seasonal campaigns
• Maintain brand consistency while ensuring compliance with Ford Motor Company guidelines
• Collaborate with leadership to develop creative campaigns that drive traffic and sales

Qualifications:
• Experience in digital marketing, social media, or content creation (automotive experience a plus)
• Bachelor's Degree in marketing, communications, journalism or related field
• Strong skills in content writing, social media management, and brand storytelling
• Experience with photo/video editing using Adobe, Canva, or similar tools
• Familiarity with SEO, SEM, and Google Analytics
• Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple projects at once
• Creative mindset with the ability to generate fresh ideas and campaigns
• A passion for customer engagement, community involvement, and automotive retail

Benefits:
• Competitive pay based on experience
• Health, dental, and vision insurance
• 401(k) retirement plan
• Paid time off and holidays
• Employee discounts
• Opportunity to be part of a long-standing, community-focused dealership
json.companyDescription:
At Loudon Motors Ford, whether you are a customer or employee, you are treated like family the minute you walk through our doors. Being a family owned dealership since 1926, family is a value that we hold near to our hearts, as we have been serving our community for almost a century. We are different than dealerships that have multiple rooftops, as we only have one and it is our home. 

We are committed to customer satisfaction, which means having knowledgeable and comprehensive sales and service departments staffed by certified professionals.

Our sales reps and service technicians undergo continuous training to keep their Ford Certified statuses. We are proud to offer paid training to keep our team up to date on the newest technologies and procedures, so that we can deliver a great experience for our customers.

Promotions most often from within here at Loudon Ford. We have staff members who have been with us for 10, 20, and even 30+ years. The opportunities to learn and grow are limitless.

At Loudon's, we are passionate about our town and surrounding community. We strive not only to support our community through the automobile-related work that we do, but in other ways that impact local families – through scholarships for students, hosting food drives, sponsoring historic building restoration projects, and numerous other charitable efforts.

It is the engagement of all our teams and leadership, both at work and in the community, that makes Loudon Motors Ford a great place to work.

We are always looking for talented people to join our team. Visit the career section on our website to learn about current openings and apply today!
json.companyBriefDescription: At Loudon's, we are committed to providing great experiences for our customers and our community. We are different than dealerships that have multiple rooftops, as we only have one and it is our home.

json.title: Analyst - Organic Growth and Content (SEO)
json.descriptionHtml:
Title: Analyst - Organic Growth and Content (Aerie / Offline)

Overview - The Analyst - Organic Growth & Content is responsible for analyzing trends and developing content with the ultimate goal of increasing the impact of organic marketing. A writer at heart, this Analyst will have a strong understanding of SEO and Answer Engine best practices, a knack for creating engaging and informative content, a strong analytical orientation and a proven track record of optimizing content for various digital platforms.

Responsibilities:

Content Creation:

  • Draft high-quality content for websites, blogs, social media, and other digital platforms, maintaining a consistent brand voice
  • Perform ongoing content gap analyses, reviewing the competitive landscape to provide trending recommendations for site improvements and content expansion that tie back to marketing strategies (audience, messaging, seasonal & annual goals).
  • Regularly audit and optimize existing content to ensure it remains relevant, up-to-date, and aligned with evolving marketing strategies.

SEO and AEO Optimization:

  • Conduct keyword and “answer engine” research to identify relevant topics and optimize content for search engines.
  • Implement on-page SEO best practices, including meta descriptions, headers, copy block creation, and internal linking.
  • Monitor and analyze content performance, adjusting strategies to improve search engine rankings and drive organic traffic.
  • Stay up-to-date with SEO trends, algorithm updates, and industry news to ensure content strategies remain effective.

Performance Analysis:

  • Use tools like Google Analytics, SEMrush, or BrightEdge to track and report on content performance.
  • Analyze metrics and make data-driven recommendations for content improvements and organic strategies.

Qualifications:

  • 2+ years of relevant experience in SEO and content writing, bonus point for retail subject matter
  • Proven ability to create engaging, well-written, well-researched content.
  • Strong understanding of SEO principles, including keyword research, on-page SEO, and link building.
  • Proficiency with SEO tools such as Google Analytics, SEMrush, BrightEdge, or similar.
  • Excellent writing, editing, and proofreading skills.
  • Familiarity with content management systems (CMS) such as WordPress.

Education:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, English, Journalism, or a related field is preferred

PAY/BENEFITS INFORMATION:

  • Actual starting pay is determined by various factors, including but not limited to relevant experience and location.
  • Subject to eligibility requirements, associates may receive health care benefits (including medical, vision, and dental); wellness benefits; 401(k) retirement benefits; life and disability insurance; employee stock purchase program; paid time off; paid sick leave; and parental leave and benefits.
    • Paid Time Off, paid sick leave, and holiday pay vary by job level and type, job location, employment classification (part-time or full-time / exempt or non-exempt), and years of service.
  • AEO may also provide discretionary bonuses and other incentives at its discretion.

PAY/BENEFITS INFORMATION:

  • Actual starting pay is determined by various factors, including but not limited to relevant experience and location.
  • Subject to eligibility requirements, associates may receive health care benefits (including medical, vision, and dental); wellness benefits; 401(k) retirement benefits; life and disability insurance; employee stock purchase program; paid time off; paid sick leave; and parental leave and benefits.
    • Paid Time Off, paid sick leave, and holiday pay vary by job level and type, job location, employment classification (part-time or full-time / exempt or non-exempt), and years of service.
  • AEO may also provide discretionary bonuses and other incentives at its discretion.

American Eagle Outfitters (NYSE: AEO) is a portfolio of unique, loved and enduring brands with merchandise assortments that consist of high-quality, on-trend apparel, intimates, activewear, accessories, and personal care products.
AEO operates stores in the United States, Canada, Mexico, and Hong Kong, and ships to approximately 80 countries worldwide through its websites. American Eagle and Aerie merchandise also is available at more than 300 international locations operated by licensees in approximately 30 countries.
AEO is an Equal Opportunity Employer and is committed to complying with all federal, state and local equal employment opportunity ("EEO") laws. AEO prohibits discrimination against associates and applicants for employment because of the individual's race or color, religion or creed, alienage or citizenship status, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, age, sexual orientation, disability, gender identity or expression, marital or partnership status, domestic violence or stalking victim status, genetic information or predisposing genetic characteristics, military or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. This applies to all AEO activities, including, but not limited to, recruitment, hiring, compensation, assignment, training, promotion, performance evaluation, discipline and discharge. AEO also provides reasonable accommodation of religion and disability in accordance with applicable law.
Applications will be accepted until the Apply Before date (if applicable), but may be extended based on applicant volume.

Job Identification 17541
Job Category Marketing
Posting Date 2025-08-27
Job Schedule Full time
Locations Pittsburgh, PA, United States

json.title: Digital Marketing Assistant
json.descriptionHtml:
Overview

The Digital Marketing Specialist is responsible for leading integrated digital marketing campaigns from concept to execution. The Digital Marketing Specialist will work with the marketing team, sales team, other internal teams, such as programmers, and vendors to launch campaigns on time and within budget.

Responsibilities

Essential Duties & Responsibilities

  • Plans and executes all digital marketing, including SEO/SEM, marketing database, email, social media, and display advertising campaigns.
  • Creates and maintains the digital marketing calendar.
  • Designs, builds, and maintains our social media presence.
  • Measures and reports performance of all digital marketing campaigns and assesses against goals (ROI and KPI).
  • Identifies trends and insights and optimizes spend and performance based on the insights.
  • Brainstorms new and creative growth strategies.
  • Collaborates with internal teams to create landing pages and optimize user experience.
  • Utilizes strong analytical ability to evaluate endtoend customer experience across multiple channels and customer touch points.
  • Collaborates with business and technology partners.
  • Evaluates emerging technologies.
  • Provides thought leadership and perspective for adoption where appropriate.
  • Sees projects through from concept to completion within assigned timelines and meets quality expectations.

Additional and/or Specific Skills

  • Proficient in MS Office Suite.
  • Strong oral and written communication skills.
  • Must be highly organized demonstrated success managing multiple tasks with a laser focus on detail as well as proven project management experience with the ability to establish and meet objectives on time.
  • Detail oriented and able to work independently or as part of a team.
  • Works well under pressure and can work extended hours to meet tight deadlines.

Education/Experience

  • Bachelor’s Degree from an accredited school in Marketing or a related field.
  • Proven digital marketing work experience.
  • Demonstrable experience leading and managing SEO/SEM, marketing database, email, social media and/or display advertising campaigns.
  • Solid knowledge of website analytics tools (Google Analytics), certification a plus.
  • Experience in setting up and optimizing Google AdWords, Facebook, and LinkedIn campaigns, Google AdWords, and Facebook Blueprint certifications a plus.
  • Experience using CRM, CMS, and ESP tools.
  • Highly creative with experience in identifying target audiences and devising digital campaigns that engage, inform, and motivate.
  • Working knowledge of media, HTML, CSS and JavaScript development and constraints.
  • Strong analytical skills and datadriven thinking.
  • Up to date with the latest trends and best practices of online marketing and measurement.
  • Experience promoting technology products a plus.

Physical Requirements

  • Long periods of sitting.
  • Call Center Noise.
  • Light to Medium phone exposure.
  • Repetitive Typing and use of a mouse.

json.companyDescription: Founded in 2002, Petrosoft LLC is a leader in cloud-based retail solutions with innovative tools that enable retail executives and managers to track sales and profits in real time. The company provides a seamless interface from the point of sale (cash registers) to popular financial tools such as QuickBooks by Intuit and Microsoft Dynamics. The solutions offered by Petrosoft can increase store automation, productivity and margins. Petrosoft maintains its headquarters in Pittsburgh, PA. The company also maintains a global network of technical development and support resources, dedicated to continually improving Petrosoft products and services.

json.title: Client Content Manager
json.descriptionHtml:
Our Life Science Connect Client Content Team plays a pivotal role in connecting our clients and our readers. The Client Content Managers are responsible for the production and optimization of content for an assigned set of customers. In addition, the Client Content Manager, manage the promotion of client content in our regular and custom newsletters. When done right, we are viewed as a partner, an extension of our clients' marketing and sales enablement team. We seek to understand, work unbelievably hard to learn what is needed, and then are relentless in our pursuit of providing real help.

Continuous investment in our products, services, and technical infrastructure has us well-positioned to achieve real results for our clients, leading to tremendous growth. To provide the highest level of support to our ever growing list of clients, we need to add a Client Content Manager to our team in Cranberry, PA.

The ideal candidate will possess a fluency in digital tools and website management frameworks such as WordPress. To be successful in this role, you will need a history of progressively responsible experience in managing digital content related to the following: content management for a highly visible website, social media, and web publishing. This position will report to our Cranberry Township, PA location

Essential Job Duties:
Client Administration and Communications:
  • Build and maintain relationships with client contacts for the collection and administration of the client's Audience Access programs
  • Assist the Business Development team with the client onboarding process, gathering company information and content
  • Ensure client Audience Access programs start on-time operate without issue throughout
  • Explain production procedures and best practices to clients and internal stakeholders
  • Help Business Development Reps identify accounts that are in need of varied or new content
Client Content Posting and Optimization:
  • Populate email newsletters monthly to ensure clients receive Audience Access inclusions. May include working directly with clients by setting deadlines and collecting specific content.
  • Align content to themed newsletter sends through the mapping of content across the upcoming month.
  • Collaborate with the editorial team to identify timely and applicable editorial content.
  • Help Business Development Reps select and schedule content for quarterly Sourcing Center newsletters and other special edition themed or product newsletters.
  • Edit and optimize all content titles and summaries within general and custom newsletters for formatting, spacing, and reader engagement.
  • Create subject lines, pre-headers, and newsletter titles for a variety of newsletter types.
  • Write and optimize unique emails as part of lead nurturing or ABM campaigns. May include web page introductions, newsletter introductions/footers, or full messaging from site partnership teams or individuals related to content themes, portfolios, or individual content pieces.
  • Project Management and reporting as needed
REQUIRED EXPERIENCE / SKILLS
  • Bachelor's degree (Equivalent work experience will be considered in place of degree)
  • 3-5 years of work experience in a content production or customer service role
  • Excellent communicator - written and verbal
  • Good proofreading and copy-editing skills
  • 1-3 years of experience with HTML, extremely comfortable with web and social media
  • Basic understand of Google search rankings
  • High attention to detail
  • Capable working alone or within a small team
PREFERRED EXPERIENCE / SKILLS
  • Technical background in communications or life sciences
  • Prior SEO training and experience
  • Knowledge of SEO copy writing
  • Experience writing original copy for the web
BENEFITS
Life Science Connect offers a generous compensation package. In addition to a competitive salary, you'll enjoy:
  • Medical/vision/prescription/dental coverage for you and your family
  • 100% company-paid short- and long-term disability insurance
  • 100% company-paid life insurance
  • 401(k) with dollar-for-dollar company match up to 6%
  • 15 vacation days and 6 personal days on day 1
  • 13 company-paid holidays
We do not offer visa sponsorship for this role

15 vacation days and 6 personal days on day 1
13 company-paid holidays

json.companyDescription:
Life Science Connect facilitates mutually beneficial connections between our audiences and strategic partners that accelerate the advancement of life-improving, life-extending, and life-saving therapies and devices. We serve a loyal, satisfied readership that demands original, compelling content with utility.

json.companyBriefDescription: Life Science Connect is a pioneering B2B media services company devoted to the creation of valuable long-term professional relationships.

json.title: Acquisitions Content Editor
json.descriptionHtml:
The landscape of the life sciences industry is as vast and varied as the professionals who work in it. Our goal is to be the most valuable resource for a specific segment of the industry – Drug Delivery – and the professionals who make their living there. They’re specialists, like us. We deliver in-depth content from authoritative authors and sources because that’s what our community demands.

The primary role of the Life Science Connect Acquisitions Editor, is the recruitment of guest content contributors with expertise in the Drug Delivery arena. The Acquisitions Editor is responsible for establishing and maintaining mutually beneficial relationships with these experts—relationships that yield high-value content for our readers. The Acquisitions Editor will be responsible for managing, editing, optimizing, and tagging contributed content for publication. This position will be at our Cranberry Township, PA location

Essential Job Duties
  • Grow and manage our network of content contributors for Drug Delivery.
  • Work with editorial and business development teams to determine target article topics, contributor profiles, and appropriate volume of coverage by topic.
  • Identify and connect with industry experts — through direct outreach (email/phone), social media, industry events, PR firms, etc. — who fit the target contributor profiles and are capable of writing original articles on target topics, developing infographics, producing written or audio/video-recorded Q&As, or other content formats deemed valuable.
  • Guide each contributor through the process of determining the highest value content topic, angle, and scope for the Drug Delivery audience.
  • Oversee the publication schedule of each contributed piece, setting appropriate deadlines for contributors, ensuring they submit all necessary materials (abstract/outline, draft, bio, headshot, images, etc.) on time, and submitting work orders for tagging and publication.
  • Edit each contributed article for grammar, adherence with our editorial style guide, general readability, SEO optimization, and reader engagement (e.g., attention-grabbing headlines).
  • Select a feature graphic for each article from our stock image catalog.
  • Grow relationships with contributors through regular interaction (email, social media, phone, etc.) to encourage their continued participation.
  • Optimize Drug Delivery newsletters.
  • Schedule all articles written by contributors for promotion in relevant newsletters.
  • Review newsletter proofs, ensuring all content is relevant, recent, grammatically correct, and optimized for performance (e.g. headline and summary check).
  • Promote contributed content to ensure engagement by target reader personas via:
      • Social media — directly and via Product Pages on LinkedIn.
      • Partner referrals.
      • Direct email messages to relevant groups, contacts, or agencies.
      • Merchandising of content across all relevant LSC sites and throughout each site in relevant buckets and centers.
Additional Job Duties
  • Support additional editorial and engagement initiatives for LSC products.
  • Leverage network of contributors for other initiatives that require SMEs (e.g. webinars and expo participants, editorial interview subjects).
  • Select articles to be aggregated into topic-specific ebooks.
  • Actively contribute in weekly editorial staff meetings.
Required Skills / Experience
  • 3+ or more years of professional work experience in editorial or communications.
  • Technical aptitude (the ability to understand and communicate technical concepts).
  • Professional presentation.
  • Willingness to interact with the business development team.
  • Business acumen.
  • Proven examples of hardiness, initiative, critical thinking, and acting with urgency.
  • Capable of establishing and maintaining professional relationships.
  • Strong written and oral communication (including demonstrated phone skills).
  • Demonstrated persuasion, influence.
  • Proficient use of: Word, Excel, E-mail, the Internet.
  • Experience hitting goals and deadlines.
Preferred Skills / Experience
    • Five or more years of professional work experience in media/communications.
    • Experience in the life sciences industry.
    • Ability to travel up to 10 days per year.
    • Excellent communicator – written and verbal.
    • Demonstrates entrepreneurial traits and characteristics.
    • Comfortable in highly structured environment.
    • Marketing knowledge, skills, and experience.
Life Science Connect offers a generous compensation package. In addition to a competitive salary, you'll enjoy:
  • Medical/vision/prescription/dental coverage for you and your family
  • 100% company-paid short- and long-term disability insurance
  • 100% company-paid life insurance
  • 401(k) with dollar-for-dollar company match up to 6%
  • 15 vacation days and 6 personal days on day 1
  • 13 company-paid holidays
We do not offer visa sponsorship for this role

To view all our job postings and showcases for some of our employees, visit: https://lifescienceconnect.com/careers/.
